name: Ensure the default firewall does not have any default rules besides http and https
body:
### Security intent

This control reduces unnecessary exposure by ensuring that **the default firewall does not have any default rules besides http and https**.

### Network condition reviewed

The assessment reviews the default firewall does not without any default rules besides http and https in Google Cloud. It focuses on rules, endpoints, listeners, or policies that make the service reachable in a riskier way than intended.

### Expected posture

Public reachability should be deliberate, documented, and tightly scoped. Administrative and data-plane paths should use private connectivity, strong TLS, explicit source ranges, and provider-native controls rather than broad internet access.
description: The default firewall does not without any default rules besides http and https
remediationDescription:
### GCP

1. Open Google Cloud Console and go to VPC network > Firewall.
2. Filter firewall rules for the default network.
3. Review rules whose names start with default-.
4. Remove default rules that allow broad ingress, especially SSH, RDP, ICMP, or internal ranges that are not required.
5. Keep only explicitly approved HTTP or HTTPS rules if the workload requires them.
6. Replace removed rules with narrowly scoped firewall rules that specify source ranges, target tags or service accounts, protocol, and port.
7. Re-sync and confirm the default network no longer contains unapproved default firewall rules.

Reference: https://cloud.google.com/firewall/docs/firewalls
